description: Escape-analysis bypass in Ion scalar-replacement for Wasm GC. AI-discovered by Claude Mythos Preview (Anthropic, 2026), rolled up under CVE-2026-6784 in Firefox 150. IsWasmStructEscaped() in js/src/jit/ScalarReplacement.cpp was tracking escape state per phi-input but missed the case where the phi itself merged an escaped and unescaped struct — Ion proved the struct non-escaping and skipped initialising its anyref slot. Subsequent Wasm GC reads observed uninitialised memory (wildptr type confusion) — under --fuzzing-safe with default tiering, the differential output between baseline and Ion exposes it; on debug+ASAN it crashes. Fix: git 1c39b127b06b 'Bug 2024918 - Fix scalar replacement' by Ryan Hunt. Reproduces with --fuzzing-safe on debug+ASAN; differential check is also valid with --ion-scalar-replacement=off (correct output -1052688063) vs default (crash).
